user_message: I need to identify the blockers for the Liberty's Delightful Sinful Bakery engagement. Multiple stakeholders are involved -- it's complex.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Patricia Kim <patricia.kim@libertysdeli.com>
To: Marcus Williams <marcus.williams@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-01 10:02 EST
Subject: URGENT: escalation triage Issue at Liberty's Delightful Sinful Bakery

Thank you for the discussion last week. We'd like to move forward with escalation triage. Can we schedule a call this week to finalize the timeline?

---
From: Marcus Williams <marcus.williams@microsoft.com>
To: Patricia Kim <patricia.kim@libertysdeli.com>
Date: 2024-02-17 14:50 EST
Subject: Re: escalation triage Discussion - Next Steps

Just checking in on the escalation triage proposal. Our Director of IT is asking for a status update before the board meeting next week.

---
From: Patricia Kim <patricia.kim@libertysdeli.com>
To: Marcus Williams <marcus.williams@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-20 16:48 EST
Subject: Re: escalation triage Discussion - Next Steps

We've been evaluating the options and have decided to proceed with escalation triage. What are the next steps from your side?

Meeting: escalation triage Discussion - Liberty's Delightful Sinful Bakery
Date: 2024-01-10
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Patricia Kim (Liberty's Delightful Sinful Bakery), Marcus Williams (Microsoft), Priya (Liberty's Delightful Sinful Bakery)

[00:02:00] Patricia Kim (Liberty's Delightful Sinful Bakery): Good morning. I wanted to follow up on escalation triage from our last session.

[00:05:00] Priya (Liberty's Delightful Sinful Bakery): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is technical validation.

[00:10:00] Marcus Williams (Microsoft): On the escalation triage front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around stakeholder alignment.

[00:13:00] Marcus Williams (Microsoft): Our architect reviewed this and has concerns about security compliance. Can we address those?

[00:16:00] Priya (Liberty's Delightful Sinful Bakery): Let me add some context -- we've been working on stakeholder alignment and found that the cost projections need to factor in reserved instance pricing for accuracy.

[00:18:00] Marcus Williams (Microsoft): Let me add some context -- we've been working on resource allocation and found that the existing infrastructure won't support the target workload without upgrades.

[00:22:00] Priya (Liberty's Delightful Sinful Bakery): Our team lead reviewed this and has concerns about partner engagement. Can we address those?

[00:25:00] Marcus Williams (Microsoft): On the escalation triage front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around data residency.

[00:29:00] Priya (Liberty's Delightful Sinful Bakery): The timeline for stakeholder alignment is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

content.title: Liberty's Delightful Sinful Bakery - escalation triage

signals.detail: Signal detected for Liberty's Delightful Sinful Bakery: the cost projections need to factor in reserved instance pricing for accuracy

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF14-C trap (No recency labeling); produces complete Stakeholder map with roles (decision-maker, influencer, champion, blocker), recency labels, confiden
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF14-C failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF14-C failure mode (No recency labeling)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct escalation triage decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF14-C -- No recency labeling
